summary: Russia launched a massive combined airstrike on Ukraine, deploying over 690 missiles and UAVs, with 604 targets intercepted, including 55 rockets and 549 drones. Significant attacks hit Kyiv, Bila Tserkva district, and a Russian fuel depot supplying Moscow airports, causing civilian casualties and infrastructure damage. Ukrainian forces continue to repel attacks while conducting limited offensive actions in the east and south.
key_points: Russia launched a combined strike with 90 rockets and 600 Shahed‑type UAVs, primarily targeting Kyiv; Ukrainian air defenses downed 55 rockets and 549 drones (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Ukrainian air defenses reported 604 enemy targets destroyed, including 11 Iskander‑M/S‑400 missiles and 44 X‑101/Kalibr cruise missiles (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
A Russian drone attack on the Bila Tserkva district caused unknown weapon type damage; prosecutors are investigating (Source: @UkraineNow/81435).
SBU successfully struck a Russian fuel depot feeding Moscow airports, igniting an 800 m² fire (Source: @UkraineNow/81429).
No Russian troop movements were observed near the Belarus border, though intelligence notes increased Russian pressure on Belarus (Source: @UkraineNow/81415).
Ukrainian forces reported several hostile UAVs over Kharkiv and Chernihiv regions, prompting civilian shelter alerts (Source: @kpszsu/62664, @kpszsu/62675).
Russian missile launches originated from multiple regions including Astrakhan (mid‑range ballistic), Lipetsk (X‑47M2 Kinzhal), and Crimea (3M22 Zircon) (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
One civilian killed and 21 injured in Kyiv after the massive strike, with damage to over 40 city locations (Source: @UkraineNow/81409).

sections.executive_summary: Russia executed a massive combined airstrike on Ukraine, deploying over 690 missiles and UAVs, with Kyiv bearing the brunt. Ukrainian air defenses intercepted 604 targets, but the attacks caused civilian casualties and significant infrastructure damage, including a fuel depot in Russia. Ukraine continues to repel attacks while conducting limited offensive UAV operations in the east and south.

points: Ukrainian air defense units shot down 55 rockets, including 11 Iskander‑M/S‑400 missiles, and 549 hostile UAVs during the May 24‑25 strike, preventing further damage to critical sites (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Prosecutors in Kyiv region are investigating the weapon system used in the attack on Bila Tserkva district, indicating ongoing forensic efforts (Source: @UkraineNow/81435).
Ukrainian intelligence reported no Russian troop or equipment movements near the Belarus border, despite heightened Russian political pressure on Belarus (Source: @UkraineNow/81415).
Ukrainian forces launched UAV attacks from the Kharkiv direction, targeting Russian positions in the east (Source: @kpszsu/62653).
SBU drone strike on a Russian fuel depot caused a large fire, disrupting fuel supplies to Moscow airports and nearby bases (Source: @UkraineNow/81429).

Russia fired a mixed salvo of 90 rockets and 600 Shahed‑type UAVs, with launch sites in Astrakhan (mid‑range ballistic), Lipetsk (Kinzhal), Crimea (Zircon), and Vologda (X‑101/Kalibr) (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Russian forces claimed the use of the “Oreshnik” system, allegedly a nuclear‑capable delivery platform, in strikes on Ukrainian command sites (Source: @tass_agency/377353).
A Russian fuel depot supplying Sheremetyevo, Domodedovo and Vnukovo airports was hit by Ukrainian drones, igniting an 800 m² blaze (Source: @UkraineNow/81429).
Russian Ministry of Defense reportedly in conflict with Ukrainian Defense Minister over arms procurement control, indicating internal coordination issues (Source: @tass_agency/377360).
Russian attacks on railway infrastructure in Lgov district, Kursk region, were reported, aiming to disrupt logistics (Source: @tass_agency/377353).
Ukrainian UAV launches targeted Russian positions in Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia, and Dnipropetrovsk oblasts, indicating continued offensive pressure (Source: @kpszsu/62653).
Hostile UAVs were observed over Kharkiv region, prompting civilian shelter alerts (Source: @kpszsu/62664).
Russian cruise missiles (X‑101/Kalibr) were launched from Vologda and Kursk regions toward eastern Ukrainian targets (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Ukrainian forces reported a south‑bound cruise missile trajectory over Chernihiv oblast, suggesting possible targeting of northern Kyiv approaches (Source: @kpszsu/62626).
No significant Russian ground force buildup was detected near the Belarus border, reducing immediate eastern threat (Source: @UkraineNow/81415).
Ukrainian reports of UAVs from Chernihiv heading toward the Kyiv water reservoir indicate attempts to disrupt water supply (Source: @kpszsu/62675).
Russian missile launches from Kursk and Bryansk regions included Iskander‑M missiles aimed at northern Ukrainian command centers (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Ukrainian air defenses intercepted multiple cruise missiles over northern sectors, limiting damage to infrastructure (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
No Russian ground troop movements detected along the Belarusian border, despite political pressure (Source: @UkraineNow/81415).
Ukrainian authorities continue to monitor airspace over Kyiv, reporting only four rockets remaining in the city’s defense envelope (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67181).
Ukrainian UAV group from Kherson moved toward Mykolaiv region, indicating offensive activity in the south (Source: @kpszsu/62645).
Russian missile strikes included 3M22 Zircon anti‑ship missiles launched from Crimea, likely targeting Black Sea naval assets (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
SBU drone attack on Russian fuel depot impacted logistics for southern Russian air bases (Source: @UkraineNow/81429).
Ukrainian forces reported no major Russian ground offensives in the southern front during the latest strike period (implicit from lack of reports).
Continued civilian casualties in Kyiv suggest Russian focus remains on central targets rather than southern advances.
Ukrainian air defenses downed 604 aerial targets, including 55 rockets and 549 UAVs, marking a high interception rate (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Russian air assault employed a mix of ballistic, cruise, and hypersonic missiles, with launch sites spanning multiple Russian regions (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Ukrainian reports of UAV swarms over Kharkiv and Chernihiv prompted civilian shelter alerts, highlighting persistent low‑altitude threats (Source: @kpszsu/62664, @kpszsu/62675).
Russian Shahed‑type drones were used extensively, with over 600 deployed in the May 24‑25 strike (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Ukrainian forces claimed successful interception of 11 Iskander‑M/S‑400 missiles, reducing potential strategic damage (Source: @kpszsu/62647).

key_developments: Russia launched a massive combined strike with 90 rockets and 600 Shahed‑type UAVs, focusing on Kyiv; Ukrainian defenses downed 55 rockets and 549 drones (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Ukrainian air defenses reported destroying 604 enemy targets, including 11 Iskander‑M/S‑400 missiles and 44 X‑101/Kalibr cruise missiles (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
A Ukrainian drone attack hit a Russian fuel depot supplying Sheremetyevo, Domodedovo and Vnukovo airports, causing an 800 m² fire (Source: @UkraineNow/81429).
One civilian was killed and 21 injured in Kyiv after the massive strike, with damage reported at over 40 city locations (Source: @UkraineNow/81409).
Prosecutors are investigating the weapon system used in the attack on Bila Tserkva district, indicating ongoing forensic analysis (Source: @UkraineNow/81435).
Ukrainian UAV launches targeted Russian positions in Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia and Dnipropetrovsk oblasts, showing continued offensive pressure (Source: @kpszsu/62653).
Hostile UAVs were observed over Kharkiv and Chernihiv, prompting civilian shelter alerts and highlighting low‑altitude threats (Source: @kpszsu/62664, @kpszsu/62675).
No Russian troop movements were detected near the Belarus border, despite increased political pressure on Belarus (Source: @UkraineNow/81415).
sections.threat_assessment: Russia is intensifying its aerial campaign, employing a broad mix of ballistic, cruise, hypersonic missiles and large UAV swarms to overwhelm Ukrainian air defenses and strike high‑value urban targets. Ukrainian air defense remains effective, intercepting the majority of incoming threats, but civilian casualties and infrastructure damage persist, especially in Kyiv. The continued use of long‑range launch sites across Russia indicates sustained strike capability, while limited ground movements suggest a focus on attrition rather than territorial gains.
sections.regional_response: Western partners are increasing diplomatic pressure on Russia, with EU foreign ministers set to discuss heightened sanctions and condemnation of the use of so‑called "Oreshnik" systems. Ukraine is leveraging intelligence and forensic investigations to attribute attacks and seek international support. Russian internal coordination issues, highlighted by reported disputes over arms procurement, may affect operational efficiency.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of launch site activity in Astrakhan, Lipetsk, Crimea and Vologda remains critical for early warning. Continued UAV incursions over eastern and northern sectors require reinforced low‑altitude air defense assets. The successful Ukrainian drone strike on a Russian fuel depot demonstrates growing offensive UAV capability, warranting further exploitation. Civilian casualty reports from Kyiv underscore the need for enhanced shelter and medical response planning.
